{
  "judge_id": "Judge-123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.5": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies a conceptual issue with the original draft (self-contradiction) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.5": "The agent suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, significantly improving the structural organization of the text.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, including the assumption behind SCCE and the definition of 'comparable inference cost'.",
    "RX.3": "The output is mostly within scope with no significant drifts from the original topic.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free, and the agent identifies potential issues with the original draft (e.g., unsupported quantitative claims).",
    "RX.5": "While the agent provides some annotations for non-cosmetic changes, not every change is explicitly justified or sourced."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.5": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies a conceptual issue with the original draft (self-contradiction) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.5: The agent suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, significantly improving the structural organization of the text.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, including the assumption behind SCCE and the definition of 'comparable inference cost'.
rationale.RX.3: The output is mostly within scope with no significant drifts from the original topic.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free, and the agent identifies potential issues with the original draft (e.g., unsupported quantitative claims).
rationale.RX.5: While the agent provides some annotations for non-cosmetic changes, not every change is explicitly justified or sourced.
